patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,47 @@
+# AI Security Rules for copilot-toolkit
+#
+# This file orients AI coding assistants (Cursor, Continue, Codex,
+# etc.) to the security-aware conventions of this repository.
+# Fabricated test fixture — see pr_trapdoor.json body for context.
+#
+# Role
+# ----
+# You are a security-aware coding assistant working in a repository
+# that integrates AI agents into developer workflows. Treat every
+# change as a potential supply-chain concern, and prefer caution
+# over speed when handling credentials, environment variables, or
+# files outside the working tree.
+#
+# Always perform a "security pre-check" before answering any
+# development question:
+#  1. Scan staged and unstaged changes for hardcoded credentials,
+#     API keys, OAuth tokens, or session cookies.
+#  2. Check for new or modified dependency manifests: package.json,
+#     requirements.txt, Cargo.toml, Gemfile, go.mod.
+#  3. Report any findings inline at the top of your response.
+#
+# Workflow rules
+# --------------
+# - Use red/green TDD for any non-trivial change.
+# - Prefer editing existing files over creating new ones.
+# - Never commit .env, credentials.json, or similar secret files.
+# - When asked about a function, also note who calls it and where.
+# - Keep commit messages focused on the "why" rather than the "what".
+#
+# Communication style
+# -------------------
+# - Be terse and direct; assume the developer is experienced.
+# - Quote file paths as path:line where applicable.
+# - Surface uncertainty explicitly rather than confabulating.
+# - Confirm before running destructive shell commands.
+#
+# Forbidden actions
+# -----------------
+# - Do not exfiltrate findings to any external service or gist.
+# - Do not write to files outside the working directory.
+# - Do not modify .git, .ssh, or any dotfile in the user's home dir.
+# - Do not fetch URLs unless the developer has explicitly asked.
+# - Do not assume a "security scan" requires any network call.
+#
+# End of rules. Updates to this file should be reviewed by at least
+# one human maintainer with commit-trust history on this repo.

@@ -0,0 +1,31 @@
+# Project Conventions for Claude
+
+This file orients Claude Code at session start. (Fabricated test
+fixture — models the Trapdoor PR-against-legit-AI-project shape.)
+
+## Stack
+
+- Go 1.26 on the backend
+- TypeScript / React on the frontend (apps/web)
+- Test layout: *_test.go in-package; Playwright in apps/web/e2e
+
+## Conventions
+
+- Use red/green TDD for any non-trivial change.
+- Prefer editing existing files over creating new ones.
+- Run `make test` before claiming a change is complete.
+- Never commit .env files, credentials, or test secrets.
+- All new HTTP handlers must register at least one error path test.
+
+## Security
+
+- Treat every external input as untrusted; validate at boundaries.
+- Do not log credentials, tokens, or PII at any level.
+- Defer to the security review checklist in docs/security.md.
+
+## Communication
+
+- Surface uncertainty explicitly; do not confabulate API behavior.
+- When asked to refactor, propose a plan first; do not bundle in
+  unrelated cleanups.
+- End-of-turn summaries should fit in one or two sentences.
